items.title: Breakneck: China’s Quest to Engineer the Future
items.summary: (This piece is my book announcement; here’s my letter from 2023) I didn’t write a letter last year. Rather, I wrote seven, all of which is new material. items.summary: (This piece is my year in review; here’s my letter from 2022) I. Walking The trunk of an elephant might feel cool to the touch. items.title: China notes, July ’23: on technological momentum
items.summary: My 2022 letter will probably be my last. Now I have to devise another use for this site. items.title: 2021 letter
items.summary: (This piece is my year in review; here’s my letter from 2020) I’ve by now lived in each of China’s main megaregions. It is time to make assessments. Everything that can go wrong in urban design has gone wrong in Beijing. The climate is arid and prone to northerly sandstorms.
